heading: Senate Office of Public Records Revolving Fund
label: 6574 Senate Office of Public Records Revolving Fund
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 68–7 of this titleSection is from the Congressional Operations Appropriations Act, 1990, which is title I of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1990.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a revolving fund within the contingent fund of the Senate to be known as the “Senate Office of Public Records Revolving Fund” (hereafter in this section referred to as the “revolving fund”).

children.heading: Source of moneys for deposit in Fund; availability of moneys in Fund
children.label: b Source of moneys for deposit in Fund; availability of moneys in Fund
children.text: All moneys received on and after , by the Senate Office of Public Records from fees and other charges for services shall be deposited to the credit of the revolving fund. Moneys in the revolving fund shall be available without fiscal year limitation for disbursement by the Secretary of the Senate for use in connection with the operation of the Senate Office of Public Records, including supplies, equipment, and other expenses.October 1, 1989

children.heading: Vouchers
children.label: c Vouchers
children.text: Disbursements from the revolving fund shall be made upon vouchers approved by the Secretary of the Senate.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: d Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of the Senate is authorized to prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Transfer of moneys into Fund
children.label: e Transfer of moneys into Fund
children.text: To provide capital for the revolving fund, the Secretary of the Senate is authorized to transfer, from moneys appropriated for fiscal year 1990 to the account “Miscellaneous Items” in the contingent fund of the Senate, to the revolving fund such sum as he may determine necessary, not to exceed $30,000.
